title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Runtime Lens: Canonical Origin
summary: Titles become unstable routes, Unicode collapses into empty slugs, case-only paths collide on deployment, old links vanish after rename, and canonical URLs point at preview origins. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the canonical origin increment in collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.
Validate old-to-new redirects for loops, chains, conflicts, missing targets, host support, and retention ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-slugs-permalinks-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_slugs_permalinks_2(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-1slr40
# Competency: ssg-canonical-origin.
# Case 4391: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_canonical_origin_1slr40(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1slr40 = "1slr40-04rls1-4391"
    assert evidence_variant_1slr40
    evidence_axes_1slr40 = (
        "reader1slr40",
        "source4391",
        "parse04rls1",
        "route1slr404391",
        "artifact43911slr40",
        "recovery04rls14391",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1slr40)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Canonical Origin: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1573 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin, keep Canonical Origin connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Canonical Origin precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 1573 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Canonical Origin model · Theory Canonical Origin, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A relative canonical link is portable and unambiguous.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-1slr40.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-1slr40(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_canonical_origin_1slr40\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?documents < 1 or routes < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?routes != documents)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?redirects < 0 or collisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?redirect_loops > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
